title: Bone Fracture Detection from Radiograph (PWDR)
sub_domain: Cortical bone discontinuity recovery from X-ray with fracture categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 PWDR Principle. Wraps L1-031 X-ray radiography with deterministic threshold rules on cortical-disruption + fragment-displacement to assign fracture categorical labels {none, hairline, complete, displaced, comminuted, intra-articular}. Universal in emergency / orthopedic radiology. UTSW Emergency Department + Orthopedic Surgery Department high-volume use.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on fracture-severity ordinal {none, hairline, complete_non_displaced, displaced, comminuted, intra_articular_complex}

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Fracture(radiograph) = step on cortical-disruption-line presence + displacement-mm + fragment-count: hairline if cortical lucency width < 1 mm; complete if discontinuity through cortex; displaced if displacement > 2 mm or angulation > 10 degrees; comminuted if fragment count >= 3; intra_articular if fracture line crosses joint surface. AO/OTA classification overlay applied per Müller AO Foundation 1996.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: cortical bone edge geometric reconstruction + per-bone fragment mask + joint-surface relationship recovered from L1-031 X-ray radiograph (multi-view: AP / lateral / oblique)
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Cortical-disruption-width and displacement are continuous on bone-edge geometric reconstruction; per-class boundaries form measure-zero hyperplanes at width = {0, 1 mm} and displacement = {0, 2 mm}. Smoothed-grading globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per percentage-point of cortical-edge detection error.

related_principles: L1-031 (X-ray radiography — radiograph attenuation analytical core, physics_core_principle)
L1-029 (CT — gold-standard sibling for complex fractures, informational)
L1-037 (DEXA — sibling bone density modality, informational)

E.description: Bone Fracture Detection (PWDR): wraps L1-031 with established orthopedic / emergency radiology grading rules. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-031): from multi-view radiograph (AP, lateral, oblique), recover cortical bone edge map, fragment segmentation, displacement vectors, joint-surface relationships. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply width / displacement / fragment-count thresholds; AO/OTA class overlay; severity bins. Difficulty tier delta = 3.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-031): radiograph -> {cortical_edge_map, fragment_masks, displacement_vectors, joint_surface}. Stage 2: severity = step(cortical_disruption_width, displacement, fragment_count); AO_OTA = decision_tree(anatomic_location, severity, articular_involvement).
E.world_state_x: Per-radiograph categorical fracture label (severity + AO/OTA classification + anatomic location).
E.observation_y: Multi-view radiograph (AP +/- lateral +/- oblique; typically 2500x3000 pixel digital radiograph at 100-200 micrometer pixel pitch).

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.attenuation_projection -> L.cortical_edge_detection -> L.fragment_segmentation -> L.fracture_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: edge_to_fragment: cortical edge geometry and fragment segmentation share underlying radiograph; coupled processing enforces topological consistency between bone fragments and disruption lines

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-031. Uniqueness conditional on adequate views. Stability dominated by overlapping_structures and growth_plate_confounder (pediatric). Joint Hadamard well-posedness established by Müller AO 1996 (foundational classification), Lindsey 2018 (deep learning fracture detection benchmark), Rajpurkar 2017 (CheXNet), Olczak 2017 (Stockholm fracture deep learning).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + edge-detection [Canny / learned] + categorical-readout [AO/OTA classifier] | end-to-end deep neural [Aidoc Bone, Gleamer BoneView] with explicit physics-informed cortical-edge regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-031 reconstruction. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(severity_hat != severity_true) <= C * |edge_displacement_error| / d(features, severity_boundary).
C.complexity: O(H * W * N_views) for stage 1; O(N_fragments) for stage 2

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Detect fracture on radiograph + classify severity'. Option-3 recast: 'Recover cortical edge + fragment segmentation via L1-031; apply width/displacement/fragment-count thresholds + AO/OTA decision tree'. Feasible because bone fractures have well-defined geometric forward model on radiograph.

joint_well_posedness_references: Müller ME et al. (1996) The Comprehensive Classification of Fractures of Long Bones (AO/OTA). Springer.
Lindsey R et al. (2018) Deep neural network improves fracture detection by clinicians. PNAS 115(45):11591-11596.
Rajpurkar P et al. (2017) CheXNet: radiologist-level pneumonia detection on chest X-rays. arXiv:1711.05225.
Olczak J et al. (2017) Artificial intelligence for analyzing orthopedic trauma radiographs. Acta Orthop 88(6):581-586.
Tiulpin A et al. (2018) Automatic knee osteoarthritis diagnosis from plain radiographs: a deep learning-based approach. Sci Rep 8:1727.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-031 recovers continuous attenuation; L1-526 wraps with fracture detection + AO/OTA classifier. Different solution_space (1D fracture class vs 2D attenuation); adds L.cortical_edge_detection, L.fragment_segmentation, L.fracture_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal in emergency / trauma / orthopedic radiology. UTSW Emergency Department + Orthopedic Surgery Department. FDA-cleared autonomous AI: Aidoc Bone, Gleamer BoneView, Annalise.ai, RBfracture, Imagen OsteoDetect. AO/OTA classification is the international standard for fracture documentation and surgical planning.
